what_this_pass_proved: Star ratings (★/★★/★★★ based on misses and overshoots) give the player a visible mastery target beyond just completing the level.
Mission Debrief with per-order log, hardest targets, and one contextual takeaway turns the end of a run into a learning moment rather than a blank score screen.
The player now has a concrete reason to replay immediately — beat my star rating and fix the specific weakness the debrief identified.
open_questions: Are star thresholds tuned correctly? If ★★★ is too easy to earn, it loses its pull as a replay hook.
Does the takeaway message feel actionable, or does it read as generic encouragement?

bugs_fixed.description: No end-of-run feedback existed — session ended with just a score. Added star rating calculation and full mission debrief overlay.

known_limits: Star rating is per-session, not per-level. A player who aces L1-L3 but struggles at L4-L5 gets a blended rating that may not reflect their actual weak point.
Debrief persistence is session-only (React ref). No cross-session comparison of star ratings or takeaways.
next_pass_first_obligation: Observe whether the star rating and debrief actually change player behavior on replay — does the player attempt to fix the weakness the debrief identified, or do they ignore the takeaway?
_note: The historical file bakery-rush-pass-2.json contains P2B-style belt tuning and diagnostic content from the same development period. This P2A record covers the star/debrief/replay-payoff work done in commit fe83609.
